questions.question_text: When must a buyer's agent in Arkansas first disclose the agency relationship to the seller or the seller's agent?
questions.correct_answer: At first contact with the other party
distractors: At the time an offer is presented
When the contract is signed
Within 3 days of showing the property
questions.explanation: A buyer's agent must disclose the agency relationship at first contact with the seller or the seller's agent. (A seller's agent instead must disclose in a timely manner, before receiving confidential information from a buyer.) Oral disclosure must later be reduced to writing.

questions.question_text: An agent makes an oral agency disclosure at first contact. What additional step is required?
questions.correct_answer: The disclosure must be put in writing
distractors: No further action is needed
The agent must file a copy with AREC
The broker must countersign the oral statement
questions.explanation: While initial disclosure may be oral, it must be reduced to writing before the party signs any document related to the transaction.

questions.question_text: Is a specific AREC form required for making agency disclosure in Arkansas?
questions.correct_answer: No, any form or procedure that complies may be used
distractors: Yes, the AREC Agency Disclosure Form is mandatory
Yes, but only for dual agency
No, because agency disclosure itself is optional
questions.explanation: AREC publishes an agency representation disclosure pamphlet, but its use is not required; licensees may use alternative forms or procedures that satisfy the disclosure rules. What matters is that disclosure is made when required and reduced to writing before the other party signs a transaction document.

questions.question_text: A buyer walks into an open house. When must the listing agent disclose that they represent the seller?
questions.correct_answer: In a timely manner, before receiving confidential information
distractors: Only if the buyer makes an offer
At the closing table
Within three days after the open house
questions.explanation: A seller's agent must disclose the agency relationship in a timely manner, early enough to avoid eliciting or receiving confidential information from the buyer. In an open-house conversation, that effectively means disclosing before the discussion goes beyond casual facts about the property.

questions.question_text: Written agency disclosure must be obtained before the other party:
questions.correct_answer: Signs any document related to the transaction
distractors: Views the property for the first time
Obtains a mortgage pre-approval
Receives the closing disclosure
questions.explanation: The written agency disclosure must be in place before the party signs any document related to the real estate transaction, including purchase contracts or listing agreements.

questions.question_text: What must every Arkansas agency agreement contain?
distractors: A guaranteed sale price
The buyer's financing terms
A minimum commission rate
questions.explanation: All agency agreements in Arkansas must include an expiration date. This protects consumers from being locked into indefinite representation agreements.

questions.question_text: A listing agreement does not include an expiration date. What is the status of this agreement?
questions.correct_answer: It violates Arkansas agency agreement requirements
distractors: It automatically expires after 90 days
It is valid for one calendar year
It is enforceable as a perpetual contract
questions.explanation: Arkansas law requires all agency agreements to include an expiration date. An agreement without one violates this requirement and may be unenforceable.

questions.question_text: An Arkansas licensee takes an exclusive listing on a handshake, with nothing in writing. Which statement is TRUE?
questions.correct_answer: The licensee has violated AREC rules
distractors: The listing is valid if two witnesses confirm it
The arrangement is proper for listings under 90 days
Oral exclusive listings are standard practice in Arkansas
questions.explanation: AREC rules require exclusive agency agreements to be in writing. An oral exclusive listing exposes the licensee to discipline. (Written non-exclusive agreements are strongly recommended but not mandatory.)

questions.question_text: Which agency agreement does AREC merely RECOMMEND, rather than require, to be in writing?
distractors: An exclusive listing agreement
An exclusive buyer agency agreement
An exclusive property management agreement
questions.explanation: AREC rules require exclusive agency agreements to be in writing and strongly recommend, but do not require, that non-exclusive agency agreements be written as well.

questions.question_text: What must a person prove in order to sue for a real estate commission in Arkansas courts?
questions.correct_answer: They were licensed when the services were performed
distractors: The commission agreement was notarized
The commission rate matched local custom
AREC pre-approved the commission amount
questions.explanation: Arkansas Code 17-42-107 requires anyone suing to collect a real estate commission to allege and prove they held a valid license when the cause of action arose. Unlicensed persons cannot recover a commission. AREC rules separately require exclusive agreements to be in writing, but the statute of frauds does not itself bar oral commission claims.

questions.question_text: An agent fails to disclose their agency representation to the other party. This is:
questions.correct_answer: A violation of the license law
distractors: Acceptable if the other party has their own agent
Only a problem if a complaint is filed
Permissible for commercial transactions
questions.explanation: Failure to disclose agency representation violates Arkansas license law and can result in disciplinary action by AREC, plus potential civil liability.

questions.question_text: What are the potential consequences of failing to disclose agency in Arkansas?
questions.correct_answer: AREC discipline and possible civil liability
distractors: A $100 fine with no further action
Only a written warning for first offense
No consequences if the deal closes successfully
questions.explanation: Failure to disclose agency can result in AREC disciplinary action (suspension, revocation, fines) and may expose the agent to civil liability from affected parties.

questions.question_text: A for-sale sign on a listed property must include:
questions.correct_answer: The firm name as recorded with AREC
distractors: The agent's personal phone number only
The property's listing price
The MLS number and tax ID
questions.explanation: All signage on listed properties must comply with firm name requirements. The firm name as recorded with AREC must appear on the sign.

questions.question_text: An agent runs an ad featuring their own name and cell number. Under AREC rules, the ad must also make obvious:
questions.correct_answer: The agent's connection with the brokerage firm
distractors: The agent's years of experience
The agent's license issue date
The agent's commission rate
questions.explanation: AREC rules allow licensee names and phone numbers in advertising only where the ad makes the licensee's connection with the principal broker's firm obvious; the firm name must appear in every ad. AREC guidance also recommends keeping the firm name at least as prominent as the licensee's.

questions.question_text: An agent who fails to disclose agency representation may face all of the following EXCEPT:
distractors: License suspension by AREC
Civil liability for damages
A monetary penalty from AREC
questions.explanation: Failure to disclose agency can result in AREC discipline and civil liability, but it does not automatically trigger criminal prosecution. Criminal charges would require separate legal proceedings.
